title: $scan
text: $scan Returns the position of a substring within a string. $scan ( Source , SearchFor ) Example: vOutput = $scan ("The quick brown fox", "quick") Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description Source String String in which the substring needs to be found. SearchFor String Substring to search for; can be a constant string or syntax string with a maximum length of 256 characters, or a regex string. Return Values Table 2. Return Values Value Meaning 0 Substring not found. > 0 Position of the substring within the string. Use Allowed in all component types. Example: Using $scan The following example finds the first occurrence of literal o in the source string. vOutput = $scan("look for o", "o") ; vOutput = 2 Copy The following example finds the literal string quick in the source string. OUTPUT = $scan ("The quick brown fox", "quick") ; OUTPUT = 5 Copy The following example finds a number (using the syntax string "#") in the source string. vOutput = $scan ("The 1st quick brown fox came 2nd.", $syntax("#")) ; OUTPUT = 5 Copy The following example finds the regex string quick in the source string. Explain this code vOutput = $scan("The quick brown fox",$regex("quick")) ; vOutput = 5 Copy The following example finds the regex string quick in the source string with case-insensitive. Explain this code vOutput = $scan("The Quick brown fox",$regex("quick","i")) ; vOutput = 5 Copy Related Links $rscan Find a substring within a string, starting from the end of the string.
